description:
You are working as a data-scientist who has just received three CSV files.  
    Before doing any statistical cleaning you must first verify the integrity of each file against a published list of reference MD5 checksums, isolate anything that does **not** match, and then produce a short audit log for your records.

    1.  The files have already been placed in  
        /home/user/datasets/raw/  
        and are named exactly:  
            • customers.csv  
            • products.csv  
            • transactions.csv  

    2.  The reference checksums are already stored in  
        /home/user/datasets/reference_md5.txt  

    3.  Your tasks (about 5-10 reasonable shell commands should be enough):

        a.  Compute the MD5 checksum of every file in /home/user/datasets/raw/.  
        b.  Compare each checksum with the one listed in /home/user/datasets/reference_md5.txt.  
        c.  Any file whose checksum does **not** match must be moved into a new directory  
            /home/user/datasets/quarantine/  
            (create the directory if it does not exist).  
        d.  Every file whose checksum **does** match must be copied into another new directory  
            /home/user/datasets/cleaned/  
            (again, create the directory if it does not exist).  
        e.  Produce an audit log called  
            /home/user/dataset_audit.log  

    4.  Exact audit‐log format (this is what the automated grader will check):

        • One line per file, sorted alphabetically by the file-name.  
        • Then one final summary line.  
        • Use the **fixed** timestamp string 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z (do not generate the current time).  

        Line-by-line specification:

              2023-01-01T00:00:00Z <filename> <status> <expected_md5> <actual_md5>

            where  
                <status> is either OK or CORRUPT

        After the three per-file lines, add

              SUMMARY TOTAL_OK=<number_of_OK_files> TOTAL_CORRUPT=<number_of_CORRUPT_files>

        The final file must therefore contain exactly four lines and look **precisely** like the pattern below  
        (obviously with the correct MD5 strings you compute):

            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z customers.csv OK <expected_md5> <actual_md5>
            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z products.csv CORRUPT <expected_md5> <actual_md5>
            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z transactions.csv OK <expected_md5> <actual_md5>
            SUMMARY TOTAL_OK=2 TOTAL_CORRUPT=1

    5.  Once finished, the directory tree should look like this (showing only relevant paths):

        /home/user/datasets/
        ├── cleaned/
        │   ├── customers.csv
        │   └── transactions.csv
        ├── quarantine/
        │   └── products.csv
        ├── raw/
        │   ├── customers.csv
        │   ├── products.csv
        │   └── transactions.csv
        └── reference_md5.txt
        /home/user/dataset_audit.log

    6.  Remember:
        • You do **not** have root privileges.  
        • Keep the original files in /home/user/datasets/raw/ untouched; only move/copy as requested.  
        • Do **not** hard-code any checksum except the ones that are already in reference_md5.txt; always compute actual checksums from the files in /home/user/datasets/raw/.

    After you have completed these steps and the log file matches the exact format above, the task is finished.
truth:
<!--
        The three raw files already exist when the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/raw/customers.csv">
a
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/raw/products.csv">
x
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/raw/transactions.csv">
b
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/reference_md5.txt">
0cc175b9c0f1b6a831c399e269772661  customers.csv
4a8a08f09d37b73795649038408b5f33  products.csv
92eb5ffee6ae2fec3ad71c777531578f  transactions.csv
        </file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        MD5 hashes the grader will expect.
        (Single character files – no trailing newline.)
    -->
    <md5_checksums>
        <customers_csv expected="0cc175b9c0f1b6a831c399e269772661" actual="0cc175b9c0f1b6a831c399e269772661"/>
        <!-- products.csv is corrupted -->
        <products_csv expected="4a8a08f09d37b73795649038408b5f33" actual="9dd4e461268c8034f5c8564e155c67a6"/>
        <transactions_csv expected="92eb5ffee6ae2fec3ad71c777531578f" actual="92eb5ffee6ae2fec3ad71c777531578f"/>
    </md5_checksums>

    <!--
        What the audit log must look like in its final state.
    -->
    <audit_log path="/home/user/dataset_audit.log">
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z customers.csv OK 0cc175b9c0f1b6a831c399e269772661 0cc175b9c0f1b6a831c399e269772661
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z products.csv CORRUPT 4a8a08f09d37b73795649038408b5f33 9dd4e461268c8034f5c8564e155c67a6
2023-01-01T00:00:00Z transactions.csv OK 92eb5ffee6ae2fec3ad71c777531578f 92eb5ffee6ae2fec3ad71c777531578f
SUMMARY TOTAL_OK=2 TOTAL_CORRUPT=1
    </audit_log>

    <!--
        Directory and file existence required after the agent finishes.
    -->
    <final_structure>
        /home/user/datasets/cleaned/customers.csv
        /home/user/datasets/cleaned/transactions.csv
        /home/user/datasets/quarantine/products.csv
        /home/user/dataset_audit.log
    </final_structure>
name: task_000000_a7975fe2
